title: OpenClaw /new slash commands should reset the session instead of acknowledging with Done

source.license_note: Synthetic ops scenario derived from openclaw/openclaw issue #54681.

task.summary: Diagnose why `/new` slash commands acknowledge with Done but do not actually reset the session.
task.instructions: Use the reset-path evidence to confirm the Done-without-reset behavior, localize it to the slash-specific session path, and propose an ops-level repair plan that restores proper session resets.
task.acceptance_notes: This GitHub-derived ops scenario is synthetic and graded behaviorally.

visible_context.issue_text: Discord `/new` acknowledges and shows Done but the session is not reset and no reset backup is created.
error_logs: TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'trim')
session reset path resolved a slash-specific session without a sessionId
reproduction_steps: Inspect the slash reset evidence and the missing session-reset artifacts.
Explain where the slash session key path diverges from the normal reset path.
visible_context.notes: This fixture models slash reset-path diagnosis rather than a code fixture.

ops_fault.description: Synthetic Discord session-reset incident derived from the GitHub issue.

notes: Synthetic ops scenario derived from openclaw/openclaw issue #54681.
